message:
fix: graph-bridge SSE test hangs by listening for abort signal

The GraphBridgeService test was flaky because the mock ReadableStream
never closed, causing the read loop to hang indefinitely after
service.stop() was called.

The problem:
- Test created a stream that enqueued one SSE event then left the
  stream open, expecting service.stop() to terminate the read loop
- However, the stream never actually closed, so reader.read() would
  hang waiting for more data even after the abort signal fired

The fix:
- Move the mock fetch inside the test to access RequestInit.signal
- Add abort signal listener: init?.signal?.addEventListener("abort",
  () => controller.close(), { once: true })
- When service.stop() aborts the fetch, the stream now deterministically
  closes and the read loop exits cleanly

This pattern ensures that SSE stream tests don't rely on implicit
timeout behavior and terminate predictably when the abort controller
signals cancellation.

Also includes bead tracker metadata updates.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
